sections.subtitle: Match Overview and Context
sections.text: Estoril Praia, sitting at 9th in the league with 42 points, host Benfica, who are in 2nd place with an impressive 78 points, in Round 32 of the league at the Estadio Antonio Coimbra da Mota in Estoril. This matchup sees a mid-table side facing a title-contending giant, promising an intriguing clash. The referee, Joao Antonio Ferreira Goncalves, averages 5.3 yellow cards and 0.32 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially feisty encounter.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last 5 matchups, Benfica have dominated with 4 wins compared to Estoril Praia's solitary victory. The most recent encounter on December 23, 2024, ended in a 3-0 victory for Benfica at home, with Zeki Amdouni earning Player of the Match honors with an 8.8 Sofascore rating. Historically, Estoril Praia have struggled to keep a clean sheet against Benfica in 20 consecutive head-to-head games, while Benfica have been the first to score in 9 out of their last 10 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Benfica come into this game on a strong run, boasting 3 consecutive wins and an unbeaten streak of 9 matches. They've also scored first in their last 9 games and have been the first-half winner in 8 of those 9. Estoril Praia, on the other hand, have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 matches. Both teams have seen over 2.5 goals in recent games, with Estoril hitting this mark in 8 of their last 10 matches and Benfica in all of their last 10.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Estoril Praia
sections.text: For Estoril Praia, Alejandro Marqués stands out as their top scorer with 10 goals, ranking 5th in the league for goals scored. Yanis Begraoui has also contributed with 8 goals and 3 assists, showing his attacking threat. João Carvalho adds creativity with 6 goals and 2 assists, while Rafik Guitane has provided 4 assists despite not scoring. Defensively, Pedro Álvaro and Félix Bacher will be crucial in trying to contain Benfica's potent attack.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Benfica
sections.text: Benfica's attack is led by Vangelis Pavlidis, who has 18 goals and ranks 2nd in the league, supported by Kerem Aktürkoğlu with 10 goals (5th in the league) and a league-leading 17 big chances created. Orkun Kökçü shines in midfield with 7 goals, 7 assists (4th in the league), and ranks 3rd league-wide with an average Sofascore rating of 7.54. Ángel Di María, with an average rating of 7.46 (5th in the league), and defender Nicolás Otamendi, with a 75.7% aerial duel win rate (5th in the league), are also key figures. Bruma adds flair with 2.3 successful dribbles per game, ranking 2nd in the league.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Stats
sections.text: Estoril Praia have scored 42 goals this season, ranking 6th in the league, but their defense has conceded 51 goals, one of the worst records at 15th. They excel in interceptions per game with 9.5625 (1st in the league) and clearances with 26.03125 per game (5th). Benfica's attack is lethal, especially late in games, with 22 goals (30.77% of their total) scored between the 76th and 90th minutes. Estoril, meanwhile, also peak late, scoring 11 goals (21.57%) in the same period. Estoril's pressing is notable, ranking 6th in possessions won in the final third per game at 4.34375.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: Estoril Praia rank 2nd in the league for fouls committed with 506, averaging 2.46875 yellow cards per game. They've seen over 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 home games, though this trend has not continued recently. Benfica, conversely, have had under 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 matches. In terms of corners, Estoril average just 3.625 per game (16th in the league) and have had under 10.5 corners in 5 of their last 6 matches, while Benfica have exceeded 10.5 corners in 4 of their last 5.
